message:
fix(acfs): harden shell-config fragment detection and runtime HOME/TARGET_HOME resolution across lib + installer + generated

Two tightly-coupled themes land together because the generated artifact
checksums in scripts/generated/internal_checksums.sh span files from
both. Splitting would put the manifest/generated checksums out of sync
with the lib/* source in the intermediate commit and trip the
acfs-manifest-drift systemd timer + dsr quality gate that exist
precisely to catch that drift.

------------------------------------------------------------------
1. Idempotent shell-config fragment detection
------------------------------------------------------------------

Previously ACFS decided whether it needed to inject PATH entries,
the zshrc loader line, the `alias agents=…`, the Go bin path, or the
externally-managed-shell-handoff block by running `grep -q "some
marker"` against ~/.zshrc, ~/.profile, ~/.zshrc.local, etc. That
substring check mis-classified several real-world configurations as
"already installed" and therefore skipped repair:

  - Comment lines mentioning the marker (e.g. `# was: ACFS PATH …`).
  - Markers appearing inside shell function bodies that had been
    moved into `.bashrc` by a dotfiles manager.
  - Quoted string fragments that happened to contain the marker.
  - Non-anchored grep matches where the legacy line had been
    partially rewritten by a third-party tool.

Replace each of those grep-based guards with a small, line-aware
awk helper that:

  - skips comment lines,
  - requires the marker to appear on an actual `PATH=` / `alias`
    line (not inside a quoted string or comment),
  - for the zshrc loader check, additionally requires that the file
    contain ONLY the two canonical ACFS loader lines, so any user
    content at all causes a backup+rewrite instead of a silent no-op
    on top of a broken loader.

Call sites (installer + manifest + onboard + acfs-global + acfs-update
+ services-setup + autofix_existing) now share that awk pattern. The
zshrc/.profile/.zprofile legacy-line repair also switches from
un-anchored `grep -Fq` + `sed` to `grep -Fxq` (full-line match) +
line-anchored `sed 's|^…$|…|'`, so a legacy line that happens to be a
suffix of a user-written line is no longer silently rewritten.

packages/manifest/src/generate.ts gets a matching fix in
`summarizeShellBlock`: it used to pick the "first non-comment line" as
the block summary, which was wrong for any install step that defined
a helper function before the real action, because the function
declaration `acfs_zshrc_is_managed_loader() {` would win. The new
logic walks `blockLines` with a shell-quote-state machine, skips
entire `{ … }` function bodies (correctly tracking single vs double
quoting so a `}` inside a string is not treated as end-of-function),
and only considers top-level statements. That keeps summary lines
stable across the new awk helpers, and `generate.test.ts` pins the
new behavior.

------------------------------------------------------------------
2. Runtime HOME / TARGET_HOME resolution hardening
------------------------------------------------------------------

Recent work (acfs lib wrappers: state_file_path_target_home, explicit
ACFS_SYSTEM_STATE_FILE short-circuit, HOME/TARGET_HOME fallback across
error-tracking/session/update/doctor/export-config) exposed a second
class of bug: sourced lib helpers were trusting an explicit
TARGET_HOME even when TARGET_USER pointed at a different account, so
running `source scripts/lib/update.sh` from a sudo context with a
stale TARGET_HOME would return the stale path as the "runtime home".

Fix, applied consistently across every affected lib that resolves a
target home (agents, autofix, autofix_version_managers, changelog,
cheatsheet, cli_tools, cloud_db, continue, dashboard, doctor,
doctor_fix, export-config, github_api, info, languages,
nightly_update, smoke_test, stack, status, support, update, user,
zsh):

  - Capture the original HOME at source time, unconditionally (not
    just when sourced), so later resolution can tell "caller's HOME"
    from "an override we injected into our own process".
  - If TARGET_USER == "root", short-circuit to /root (same rule as
    before but now pulled to the top of every resolver so every
    subsequent branch can assume non-root).
  - Only trust explicit TARGET_HOME if (a) it's a non-root absolute
    path that actually exists AND (b) either TARGET_USER is unset OR
    TARGET_USER matches the current user. Otherwise, prefer the
    passwd entry for TARGET_USER, and only fall back to the explicit
    TARGET_HOME if every other resolver fails.
  - Add `X_current_home_acfs_candidate` / `X_trusted_lookup_acfs_home`
    helpers in doctor/info/status/user/cheatsheet that verify
    ACFS_HOME corresponds to the resolved target home (via
    `X_acfs_home_matches_home`) before using it to locate project
    artifacts. Previously a stale/inherited ACFS_HOME could cause
    these probes to read config from a different user's `~/.acfs`.
  - Hoist the JSONL `loaded_jsonl_hash` capture in update.sh to fire
    before the import loop so any concurrent JSONL rewrite surfaces
    as a staleness error instead of silently overwriting.
  - Export a process-wide `ACFS_INITIAL_ENV_HOME` set once at the top
    of update.sh so child processes can recover the caller's HOME
    even after update.sh reassigns HOME mid-execution.

Test coverage:

  - tests/unit/lib/test_update.bats: two new bats cases
    (`source-time HOME repair does not make stale TARGET_HOME explicit`
    and `…prefers TARGET_USER passwd over stale TARGET_HOME`) that
    source update.sh with stale TARGET_HOME and assert the resolver
    returns the current-user passwd home, not the stale path. Also
    pre-creates `$stale_home` so the first existing-path guard
    actually fires.
  - tests/unit/lib/test_user.bats: new case pinning the same rule
    for user.sh's runtime-home resolver.
  - tests/unit/test_changelog_export_status.sh: new
    `test_services_setup_repairs_stale_explicit_target_home_from_passwd`
    to pin services-setup.sh's init_target_context behavior against a
    mocked passwd resolver.
  - tests/unit/test_doctor_fix.sh: coverage for the new
    `_acfs_doctor_trusted_lookup_acfs_home` path.

------------------------------------------------------------------
3. Generated artifacts + manifest hash
------------------------------------------------------------------

scripts/generated/{install_shell,install_acfs,install_stack,doctor_checks,manifest_index,internal_checksums}.sh
are regenerated from the new manifest.yaml + lib files by
`cd packages/manifest && bun run generate`. Matching SHA256 updates
land in internal_checksums.sh and ACFS_MANIFEST_SHA256 in
manifest_index.sh so the curl|bash installer's integrity verification
passes and the acfs-manifest-drift timer + dsr quality gate report
clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

+test_fix_path_ordering_repairs_stale_marker_missing_atuin() {
+    setup_test_env
+
+    local zshrc="$HOME/.zshrc"
+    echo "# Initial zshrc" > "$zshrc"
+    echo "" >> "$zshrc"
+    echo "# ACFS PATH ordering (added by doctor --fix)" >> "$zshrc"
+    echo 'export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"' >> "$zshrc"
+
+    start_autofix_session >/dev/null || {
+        echo "  Failed to start autofix session"
+        cleanup_test_env
+        return 1
+    }
+
+    fix_path_ordering "path.ordering" >/dev/null 2>&1
+
+    if ! grep -Fq '$HOME/.atuin/bin' "$zshrc"; then
+        echo "  Stale PATH ordering block was not repaired with Atuin path"
+        cat "$zshrc"
+        end_autofix_session >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
+        cleanup_test_env
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    if grep -Fxq 'export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"' "$zshrc"; then
+        echo "  Stale PATH ordering export was left behind"
+        cat "$zshrc"
+        end_autofix_session >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
+        cleanup_test_env
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    if [[ $FIX_APPLIED -ne 1 ]]; then
+        echo "  FIX_APPLIED should be 1 for stale marker repair, got $FIX_APPLIED"
+        end_autofix_session >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
+        cleanup_test_env
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    end_autofix_session >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
+    cleanup_test_env
+    return 0
+}

+test_fix_acfs_sourcing_ignores_commented_loader_mention() {
+    setup_test_env
+
+    local zshrc="$HOME/.zshrc"
+    echo "# Initial zshrc" > "$zshrc"
+    echo "# source ~/.acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc" >> "$zshrc"
+
+    local acfs_zshrc="$HOME/.acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc"
+    echo "# ACFS config" > "$acfs_zshrc"
+
+    start_autofix_session >/dev/null || {
+        echo "  Failed to start autofix session"
+        cleanup_test_env
+        return 1
+    }
+
+    fix_acfs_sourcing "shell.acfs_sourced" >/dev/null 2>&1
+
+    if ! grep -Fxq '[[ -f ~/.acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc ]] && source ~/.acfs/zsh/acfs.zshrc' "$zshrc"; then
+        echo "  Active ACFS source line was not added when only a comment mentioned acfs.zshrc"
+        cat "$zshrc"
+        end_autofix_session >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
+        cleanup_test_env
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    if [[ $FIX_APPLIED -ne 1 ]]; then
+        echo "  FIX_APPLIED should be 1 for commented loader repair, got $FIX_APPLIED"
+        end_autofix_session >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
+        cleanup_test_env
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    end_autofix_session >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
+    cleanup_test_env
+    return 0
+}
